mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal expanding economic output, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The relationship is persistent across all phases of the business cycle.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO MoM change on 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window, or the average 20-day return following positive INDPRO changes is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, monetary policy is at maximum accommodation. Historical episodes show equities rally over the following quarter as ultra-low rates encourage risk-taking and the real economy begins to respond, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS < 0.25, or the mean return is not reliably above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both GDP (demand) and industrial production (supply) are contracting simultaneously on a year-over-year basis, the economy is in a severe synchronized downturn. Such periods have historically been followed by a brisk recovery fueled by policy stimulus and pent-up demand, yielding positive 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 YoY growth < 0 AND INDPRO YoY growth < 0
failure_condition: In these synchronized contraction episodes, the average 20-day forward SPX return is negative or not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96, p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is below 2% year-over-year, month-over-month increases in retail sales signal genuine consumer demand recovery without imminent inflation risk. In this low-inflation regime, rising retail sales are positively correlated with S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as revenues improve without policy tightening.
failure_condition: In periods with CPI YoY < 2%, the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS MoM change and 20-day forward returns is less than 0.05 or negative, violating the hypothesized positive relationship.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts drop below 600 thousand units annually, the housing market is deeply depressed. Historically, such extremely low levels have marked a trough in residential investment, and the subsequent recovery cycle produces positive equity returns over the next quarter as housing-related demand rebounds.
failure_condition: The average 60-day SPX return following HOUST < 600 is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05), or the strategy of buying at this signal has a Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over the backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls directly reflect expanding labor demand. Positive payroll growth strengthens household income and corporate earnings expectations, leading to immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to the improving employment picture.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS MoM changes are negative in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not statistically greater than zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth turns positive while the unemployment rate remains elevated (above 8%), the economy is in a jobless recovery phase. Consumer spending improves before labor market healing, and markets anticipate eventual job gains, producing positive 20-day forward returns as sentiment lifts.
failure_condition: In periods with RSAFS positive and UNRATE >= 8%, the average 20-day forward SPX return is negative or not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.96).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production grows more than 0.5% month-over-month while the federal funds rate is simultaneously declining, the economy benefits from both expansionary supply and monetary accommodation. This combined signal historically drives strong positive equity returns over the following 20 trading days as profits and valuations rise together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth > 0.5 AND FEDFUNDS MoM change < 0
failure_condition: The joint signal produces 20-day forward returns that are not significantly positive (average return < 0 at 95% confidence), or the hit rate (positive returns) is below 55%.
